prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
# Translations of "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now."

## Spanish
**"El mejor momento para sembrar una higuera fue hace veinte años; el segundo mejor momento es esta mañana."**

*Note: I chose "higuera" (fig tree) because in Spanish-speaking cultures the fig tree is a classic symbol of patient cultivation and generational legacy, and "esta mañana" (this morning) gives urgency a tangible, everyday feel.*

## Japanese
**「種を蒔くのに一番良かったのは二十年前。次に良いのは、今日の夕暮れ前だ。」**
*(Tane o maku no ni ichiban yokatta no wa nijū-nen mae. Tsugi ni yoi no wa, kyō no yūgure mae da.)*

*Note: I shifted "plant a tree" to "sow seeds" (種を蒔く), a deeply resonant image in Japanese expressing humble beginnings, and anchored "now" to "before today's dusk" to evoke the Japanese sensitivity to fleeting time and seasonal urgency.*
